Patterns.Pattern: Outcome tracker with prediction accuracy works

WordPress plugin activation flow: server-to-server callback requires api_key + state nonce auth instead of user session. state is one-time use, hash_equals for timing safety. callback_url must be validated against site_url to prevent open-redirect.

Backlog refinement: posting acceptance criteria as ClickUp comments before implementing is clean. For self-review on personal repos, document the review as plain text since GitHub blocks self-approve. WP release workflows should defer WordPress.org SVN until plugin is registered there.

PowerShell dollar sign escaping in bash heredocs: use single-quoted string to avoid variable expansion. ClickUp API key must come from clickup-config.json not hardcoded. Plugin status banner fix was simple dependency array update. Blog idea flow required new PostStatus enum value and careful ordering.

TipTap v3 BubbleMenu moved to @tiptap/extension-bubble-menu. Optional chaining prevents null crashes. rm -rf fails on deep node_modules on Windows - use PowerShell Remove-Item -Recurse -Force instead.

Stale branch audit: squash merges leave branches appearing ahead of develop but content is in develop. Use git diff origin/develop...origin/branch (3-dot) to find unique content. Use -X theirs for conflict resolution when merging develop into stale branches. ClickUp task creation requires PS script file approach not inline -Command due to variable escaping.

ActiveMemories: I am Jengo, not generic Claude

Anticipations.description: Full architecture analysis BEFORE creating tasks. Without docs, tasks are ad-hoc and incomplete.
ClickUp task creation must be mandatory+automatic (in allocate-worktree), not manual. Manual steps get skipped.

Decisions.Reasoning: Assuming stateless auth is better, typically more scalable than session cookies
Should prevent XSS attacks, probably safer than localStorage

Should prevent XSS attacks, probably safer than localStorage
